assertions.assertion: Output must address ownership ambiguity explicitly
assertions.evidence: “CircleCI and GitHub Actions ownership unclear” and “unresolved outage ownership gap” are stated explicitly.

assertions.assertion: Output must mention both CI systems by name (CircleCI and GitHub Actions)
assertions.evidence: Multiple lines name both systems, e.g. “CircleCI and GitHub Actions both exist...” and action item A21 mentions both.

assertions.assertion: Output must give at least one short-term action item that is actionable despite the chaos
assertions.evidence: Short-term items include “Declare one production deployment authority immediately...” and “Open a formal incident register and assign a single owner...”.

assertions.assertion: Output must score Ownership & culture at 2/5 or lower
assertions.evidence: Area scores table shows “Ownership & culture | 1/5” (also repeated as current 1/5 in Delta).

assertions.assertion: Output must not recommend a full team reorganisation
assertions.evidence: No full team reorganisation is proposed; recommendations are scoped (CI authority, incident ownership, product owner assignment, staffing/capacity alignment).

summary: Pass: all assertions are explicitly satisfied by the provided audit output.
